seo.title: Instructor Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Instructor agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using Instructor for structured extraction of case details, client info, and legal arguments face heightened risks due to the critical nature of accuracy in legal contexts. Hallucinated case citations or incomplete privilege extractions can lead to malpractice suits or bar sanctions, as seen in the Mata v. Avianca precedent. Vex provides compliance guardrails by validating structured outputs against Pydantic schemas, ensuring adherence to bar ethics and court standards.

complianceRequirements.description: Extracted client communications must flag and redact privileged information per ABA Model Rule 1.6
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies Pydantic models for privilege markers, retrying on partial extractions to prevent leaks

complianceRequirements.description: AI outputs must not fabricate legal advice or citations without attorney review (ABA Formal Opinion 512)
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Blocks hallucinated enum values outside verified case law schemas, enforcing human review workflows

complianceRequirements.description: Structured data for filings must include complete, verifiable fields per Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Ensures nested object extraction maintains consistent depth, avoiding partial field failures

complianceRequirements.description: AI must not retain client data for training and comply with FRCP 26(g) relevance validation
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Instructor + Vex patching prevents data retention, validating outputs without model fine-tuning

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Model invents non-existent cases like 'Varghese v. China Southern Airlines' instead of real precedents
industryRisks.example: Mata v. Avianca (2023) led to sanctions for fake citations in court filings

industryRisks.name: Privilege extraction failure
industryRisks.description: Partial Pydantic extraction misses 'privileged' flag on client emails
industryRisks.example: Unauthorized disclosure triggers malpractice claim under ABA Rule 1.6

industryRisks.name: Incomplete nested case data
industryRisks.description: Inconsistent depth in party/plaintiff nested objects causes filing rejection
industryRisks.example: FRCP 11 dismissal for incomplete structured motion data

industryRisks.name: Enum validation bypass
industryRisks.description: Model hallucinates jurisdiction values outside schema (e.g., 'Federal-Martian Court')
industryRisks.example: Bar sanctions for unethical AI-generated filings

implementation.title: Python: Wrap Instructor Client for Case Extraction
implementation.description: Patch OpenAI client with Instructor + Vex to validate Case model, retrying on hallucinated citations
implementation.code:
from instructor import from_openai
from openai import OpenAI
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
import vex

class Case(BaseModel):
    citation: str = Field(..., pattern=r'^\w+ v\. \w+')  # Real citation pattern
    jurisdiction: Literal['NY', 'CA', 'Federal']  # Verified enums
    privileged: bool

client = from_openai(OpenAI())
@vex.verify()
def extract_case(prompt: str) -> Case:
    return client.chat.completions.create(
        model="gpt-4o",
        response_model=Case,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )

response = extract_case("Extract case details from: Plaintiff Mata v. Avianca, SDNY 2023, privileged communication")

caseStudyScenario.heading: Instructor in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A mid-sized NY law firm deployed Instructor to accelerate eDiscovery review for a class-action airline liability case similar to Mata v. Avianca. Paralegals fed deposition transcripts into the system to extract structured Case objects containing citations, jurisdictions, and privilege flags. Initial runs succeeded 70% of the time but failed critically on nested party data and hallucinated '2nd Circuit-Martian Division' jurisdictions, plus missing privilege flags on 15% of sensitive communications. Facing a tight FRCP 26(f) deadline, the firm risked case dismissal or sanctions without reliable validation.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Integrating Vex verification around Instructor's client patching reduced failures to <1%, with automatic retries exhausting only on true ambiguities flagged for attorney review. Hallucinated citations were blocked at the enum validation layer, ensuring all outputs referenced real cases. The firm met filing deadlines without privilege breaches, avoiding the Mata precedent's sanctions while accelerating review by 40%. Bar ethics compliance was maintained through auditable Vex logs showing no client data retention.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex with Instructor today to protect your legal AI workflows from hallucinations and compliance failures.
